title: aristolochiaceae
intro: The aristolochiaceae rank refers to the botanical family, which is a taxonomic rank used to classify and group plants that share similar characteristics and evolutionary relationships. The Aristolochiaceae family comprises plants commonly known as birthworts or snakeroots, which are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. These plants are known for their unique, often twisted or cordate leaves and their ability to thrive in a variety of environments. The Aristolochiaceae family is comprised of approximately 480 species, spread across 10-15 genera.
